original text:
Deep Run High School PTSO will hold their 12th annual Summer Camp Fair from 5:30 p.m. to 8 p.m. in Commons 1 at the school, 4801 Twin Hickory Road.
A Henrico County high school has received national acclaim.
The 17-year-old student ran off the road and hit a traffic light pole at the corner of Nuckols Road and Lake Brook Drive around 1:30 a.m., according to police.
The Community Foundation has recognized 10 Henrico County teachers for outstanding instruction for 2018.
The stream will be available on desktop computers as well as mobile devices and tablets, and will be replayed on HCPS-TV.
Four Henrico County high schools were listed among the Top 35 public high schools in all of Virginia, according to U.S. News & World Report.
The community has come together to support Deep Run High School football coach Chad Hornik and his family after a devastating fire.
NBC12 reporters are covering Election Day from several locations and following several stories.
10 locales around the Richmond region with a unique identity.
You can't walk out of the gym without feeling better," Terra Balla, an exceptional education instructor at Deep Run and organizer of the league, said.
Three Central Virginia high schools are in the top 20 of a recent U.S. News & World Report of "Best Virginia High Schools."
The first week of school, drivers expect a little more traffic on the roads, but an area man said he didn't expect the 'chaos' he ran into at a Henrico library.
Sixteen swimmers from NOVA of Virginia Aquatics in Henrico recently signed collegiate letters of intent to continue their swimming careers. They are: • Caroline.
A Richmond high school's robotics team will compete this weekend against more than 30 teams from the Washington region.
The No. 9 hitter came through in the bottom of the eighth for a 6-5 victory that propels the Bruins into Saturday's state championship game at 2 p.m..
Americans take to the polls on Tuesday to vote for national, state and local representatives.
Chase Bernstine and Nina Sorkin, the 2016 All-Metro players of the year, are back for their senior seasons.
HENRICO, Va. - Henrico County Public Schools will provide live streaming video again this year of all HCPS graduations taking place at VCU's Siegel Center.
For the third year in a row the Halifax County High School will play in the Virginia High School League Class 5 State Golf Tournament.
GLEN ALLEN - Prince George Baseball (20-3) avenged its regional championship loss to the Deep Run Wildcats (12-10) 7-3 in the Class 5 state semifinals.
Hermitage's defense looks stout. Highland Springs has new faces everywhere. L.C. Bird looks poised for another strong rushing attack.
Chad Hornik scored noteworthy victories, both on and off the field, as football coach at Richmond's Thomas Jefferson High School from 2012 to 2015.
Reaching a crescendo with a wild penalty-kick win in the state quarterfinals, the Cox boys soccer team appeared drained three days later in the semifinals.
The teenager was considered endangered, according to police.
Henrico County School officials are aware that the roof of Hermitage High School needs to be fixed and said they plan to present something to the board soon.
Henrico county schools could soon align with other large school systems that give letter grades based on a 10-point scale.
Thomas Jefferson High School first-year football Coach Chris Mitchell sees a clear path to the 3A East Region playoffs for the Richmond school.
Diana Winston represents the Three Chopt District on the Henrico School Board.
CHARLOTTESVILLE  -  It made sense, really, that the kid who hated to sit still would become a runner.
Cameron Smook, a senior majoring in mining and minerals engineering in the College of Engineering, graduated from Deep Run High School in Glen Allen.
Royals routed Matoaca 10-0, will continue to the Region 5A South playoffs.
Without his lung transplant, Tucker Gordon likely would have died two years ago.
Peyton Walsh reached a career milestone Feb. 6, when he became the 13th wrestler in Navy history to reach the career 100-win mark.
The Adelphi Women's Soccer Team is adding nine new players for the 2017 season that includes two Long Islanders.
The Radford baseball team seems headed to the NCAA tournament for the first time in its history.
We have listed the latest news headlines that include Deep Run High	. This site has been created to offer a place for DRHS	alumni to reconnect and share memories.
In addition to these news headlines, you may also want to join the Deep Run High alumni directory and check out the yearbook listings.

Available for iPhone, iPad, Samsung Galaxy and Samsung Note. Customize with monogram or photos.
Now it's easy to create unique, custom smartphone and tablet cases that stylishly protect your device - in just minutes! Every one of our cases is a cut above because we use our exclusive Color Integration TechnologyTM and make them with high-quality durable yet flexible materials. From renowned designer patterns to your own favorite photos, every MyCustomCase product is totally customizable. Plus, you'll love our lightning-fast production and shipping on all	Custom iPhone, iPad, Samsung Galaxy, and Samsung Note Cases.
MyCustomCase.com cases combine state-of-the-art technology with the latest design styles and directions. Our easy-to-use online tools make it simple to create completely customizable mobile phone cases for the iPhone, Samsung Galaxy, and Samsung Note with names, monograms and initials or favorite photos.
Whichever design or case style you choose - be it a bold graphic print Slim Case or a stunning photo Extra Durable - you can rest assured that every cover from MyCustomCase.com will totally surround and help to protect your mobile phone or device ... in style!
MyCustomCase.com is committed to creating an inclusive experience for people with disabilities. If you need assistance, please call 1-888-565-8992.
*Sale ends 11:59:59 pm Pacific Time on April 22, 2019.

original text:
This lecture explores Richard Bauckham's argument (i.e. how names of persons in the Gospels/Acts compare to Tal Ilan's lexicon of names from that time period).
​An analysis of textual problems around the 'pericope adulterae' and an argument for taking it seriously.
This message discusses implications of archaeological discoveries concerning the Pool of Bethesda and evidence if may have been an 'Asclepeion'.
Heroin addiction, worship, and living waters - a fresh reading of John 4 in light of linguistic and contextual features: "Behold, the fields are white for harvest!" (John 4:35).
What is the goal of your life, and how does it relate to the grace of God? A discussion on the relationship of justification and sanctification in the context of Phil. 3:12-4:1.
These two debates cover areas of morality and the historical case for the resurrection of Jesus.
The first debate took place in Seattle Town Hall, whereas the second debate took place at a smaller, local venue.
My father's testimony and his debate with Bob Seidensticker.

This lecture explores Richard Bauckham's argument (i.e. how names of persons in the Gospels/Acts compare to Tal Ilan's lexicon of names from that time period).
 An analysis of textual problems around the 'pericope adulterae' and an argument for taking it seriously.
This message discusses implications of archaeological discoveries concerning the Pool of Bethesda and evidence if may have been an 'Asclepeion'.
Heroin addiction, worship, and living waters - a fresh reading of John 4 in light of linguistic and contextual features: "Behold, the fields are white for harvest!" (John 4:35).
What is the goal of your life, and how does it relate to the grace of God? A discussion on the relationship of justification and sanctification in the context of Phil. 3:12-4:1.
These two debates cover areas of morality and the historical case for the resurrection of Jesus.
The first debate took place in Seattle Town Hall, whereas the second debate took place at a smaller, local venue.
My father's testimony and his debate with Bob Seidensticker.

original text:
Nestled in the heart of the Pisgah National Forest, The Retreat at Parson's Glen is only 3 miles from Penland School of Crafts and minutes from Spruce Pine and Bakersville, NC. You can be in Asheville, Boone, Blowing Rock or Banner Elk within an hour.
​We're excited to share more of our Quaint Mountain Retreat with you, so touch base and let us know how we can help!

 We're excited to share more of our Quaint Mountain Retreat with you, so touch base and let us know how we can help!

original text:
Many thanks to Dermot Dolan for this guest entry. Beautiful photos!
I walked from St-Jean-Pied-de-Port to Irun last year as part of my Camino walk. On the 23 Aug this year (2012) I started the GR10 in St Jean and finished (because of other commitments) in Bolquère on Thursday 27 Sept. I walked alone.
It was absolutely amazing. I've never done mountains before and am less than three years doing any walking. Came very close to finishing it. I had intense heat, lightning, a fierce wind of almost 120km per hour on the ridge just before Refuge de Rulhe (it lasted a terrifying three hours), cold, hail, sleet, snow and ice.
I did not use a tent but brought a small gas stove and stayed in about 6 cabanes, some refuges and the rest gîtes and a hotel one night. Cost excluding flight but including train to Cahors and Carcassonne to meet friends 1580 euros for six weeks.
It was tough going but worth it I will finish it next year and then may turn around and do the GR11 in opposite direction from east to west.
I used a harness to wear a Nikon d80 SLR camera, so I am now starting the long edit of many photos. May have exhibition next year with the photographs I have done earlier in the year of the Via de la Plata and Le Puy en Veley to Cahors.
Yes I took your advice and did the Vignemale loop, it was great.
Thanks again for your advice and encouragement.
This entry was posted on Wednesday, December 5th, 2012 at 4:11 pm	and is filed under Uncategorized. You can follow any responses to this entry through the RSS 2.0 feed. You can leave a comment below, or trackback from your own site.

original text:
In order to build a corporate culture and forge bonds with your partners and customers, we always believe that people need to be part of a common event.
The success of any event organized in France lies in the commitment to running the event.
The outcome of an event will be successful if the event planner inculcates professionalism and expertise skills. The event planner is expected to have a sense of responsibility.
Thus he should use a distinctive, innovative approach that strives to add value to the event.
The budget is an overall determinant of the event quality.
1.	WНАТ ТYРЕ ОF ЕVЕΝТ?
An event planner can't organize an event successfully without knowing the type of event.
The event planner needs to know if the corporate event is a meeting, a conference, a business fair or a product launch. It could equally be an alumni event or a recruitment fair.
Whatever the case, knowing the exact nature of the event will enable the event planner to automate manual tasks. Such knowledge will also help to estimate the budget thereby facilitating preparations across the various departments.
2.	WНАТ RЕGІОΝЅ НАЅ YОUR РRЕFЕRЕΝСЕ?
If you want to host an event in France, it is also essential to know the right region.
Paris and Riviera-Monaco are some of the best destinations to host events in France. The corporate event planner or French DMC still needs to make a decision on where the event will take place. He may decide that the event will hold at the heart of a prestigious hotel, in a boat or even on the roof of a restaurant.
You should take note that France and most of its big cities have unique event sites. However, an exceptional opportunity to invite professionals for a meeting or workshop could change the scene. It is advisable to choose service providers in the city where the event will hold. For instance, if you settle for Paris, all your service providers will likely come from there too. It's cheaper this way and it's easy to estimate a budget after choosing the preferred region.
We believe that France is one of the best destinations in the world. The diversity of cities, landscapes, history and gastronomy are strong motto for a majority of our clients.
3.	WНАТ ЅТYLЕ ОF VЕΝUЕ?
France is a multicultural country so selecting a venue style for an event is quite easy. France offers a range of modern, traditional, and even trendy event styles. Therefore, the event planner should reach out to vendors and suppliers as soon as he books the venue. Arranging a kick-off meeting with these people will prevent last minute effort duplications.
Also, last minute arrangements can cost extra if the vendors are forced to adjust their schedules to accommodate your needs. It is imperative to choose an event style that matches your budget. You should not opt for a very high style which you can't afford. Also, ensure that everything is available for the event dates. Once you make the decision about the style, it will be easy for you to draw its budget.
4.	WНАТ РЕRІОD ОF ТНЕ YЕАR DО YОU РLАΝ ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ?
The period of the year is another crucial factor to put into considering while estimating the budget for an event in France. France has warm and cold seasons, and the event manager can advise which period is suitable for a particular event.
If you are planning an event in Paris, it's better to do it between June and October. The reason is that this period is relatively warm and your guests will be comfortable. Also, summer activities are quite different from winter activities. Therefore different seasons will probably have different costs.
Each period has his key features. The local Dmc is your best adviser to select the appropriate destination regarding the period of the year.
5.	НОW LОΝG ЅНОULD ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ ВЕ?
The duration of the event is a crucial factor to consider when planning an event in France. The length of the event will help the event organizer to better plan for the event. Knowledge of the duration will also guide you in drawing a practical budget for the event.
It is advisable for the French DMC to check out affordable venues and adjust dates to minimize cost. More so, the event planner can play with the event date. For example, you could start the conference in the afternoon so that you don't spend on breakfast and lunch.
6.	DО YОU RЕQUІRЕ АССОМОDАТІОΝ?
Accommodation is another important consideration to make when drawing a budget for an event in France. You need to decide whether or not you will provide accommodation for your guests. You might want to minimize cost by opting for twin bedrooms in the hotel.
The advantage is that two attendees get to share a single room. Whatever the case, just have in mind that many hotels in France offer very competitive and attractive group rates.
The majority of corporates go for 4 star properties.
You will find a larger choice and the best deals for groups in this category.
7.	НОW МАΝY РЕОРLЕ ТО ВUDGЕТ?
Knowledge of the number of people you are expecting will help you come up with a reasonable budget. If you are expecting many people, you can try to negotiate a package price for certain services. For instance, when it comes to transporting your guests, you may ask to be billed per bus instead of per person.
You might also opt for vans instead of buses. Such attitudes could help you reduce expenditures. The event planner should also be able to compare the costs per person and the expenditures per groups of attendees.
8.	DО YОU REQUIRE А ЅІТЕ VІЅІТ?
А sіtе іnsресtіоn іs thе bеst wау tо mаkе уоur fіnаl dесіsіоn.
The visits are organized by the local dmc to potential event venues.
The purpose of the visits is to check if the hotel and venues facilities are up to the standards for your corporate event.
Usuаllу, thе sіtе іnsресtіоn tаkеs оnе оr twо dауs. Vеndоrs аррlу dіsсоunt rаtеs for accommodation and transportation if you stay longer.
9.	НОW СRЕАТІVЕ АΝD ТАІLОR МАDЕ ЅНОULD ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ ВЕ?
Wе knоw thаt уоu wаnt thе bеst еvеnt оut thеrе, but thе rеаlіtу іs thаt nоt еvеrуоnе іs ехресtіng а mаstеrріесе. Ѕоmеtіmеs thе еvеnt јust hаs tо wоrk.
Hence, the French DMC may bring suggestions that will cause thrills and excitements. But you should have in mind that such creative ideas always increase the overall cost.
We know that you want the best event out there, but the reality is that not everyone is expecting a masterpiece.

9. НОW СRЕАТІVЕ АΝD ТАІLОR МАDЕ ЅНОULD ТНЕ ЕVЕΝТ ВЕ?
Wе knоw thаt уоu wаnt thе bеst еvеnt оut thеrе, but thе rеаlіtу іs thаt nоt еvеrуоnе іs ехресtіng а mаstеrріесе. Ѕоmеtіmеs thе еvеnt јust hаs tо wоrk.
Hence, the French DMC may bring suggestions that will cause thrills and excitements. But you should have in mind that such creative ideas always increase the overall cost.
We know that you want the best event out there, but the reality is that not everyone is expecting a masterpiece.

original text:
Today we highly value personal care, but until the early 18th century, a man could in his life never to dive into the water. Many people believed that bathing - this is an unhealthy thing, and "soak" his body in water, especially hot, allows various diseases and infections to enter the body. Even if people still like to take a bath, he did it in the clothes! This habit persisted until the late 19th century.
Until the 1880s people did not think about creating a deodorant, despite the fact that most of them most of the time had very bad smell, but no discomfort from this, they have not experienced. Rich tried to mask the odor lots of spirits, but today, everyone knows that this method does not work.
It is worth noting that even in the 9th century scholar Ziryab (Ziryab) proposed the idea of ​​a deodorant for underarms, but it did not take. Up until 1888 there was nothing like that. That was the year has begun mass production of the first deodorant that had phenomenal success in the west. These deodorants are available today and are marketed under the brand name "Mom".
Women did not care for the hair on the body. In the Western world removal of hair from the body was not common until the 1920-ies. There are, of course, some countries, women are still not doing anything with their hair on the body. However, it is not worth talking about it right now.
The house in that period of time the ill-smelling natural feces of a human organism. Then there was no running water, and most of the people there were excreting in a pot, which might have been left until someone did not dare to throw out its contents to the nearest window. Later pots have been preserved in some of the houses, but had already been created by open toilets. However, even in the Victorian era, pots were used as emergency toilet in the night.
Toilet paper was invented in the late 18th century, and until then people used the materials at hand. The rich could afford the luxury of a piece of cloth to wipe yourself. Poor people used old rags, leaves, moss and ... hands! Even the ancient Romans were better - they are used for this purpose cloth wound around a stick, and then immerse it in a bucket of water.
